headline: Steganographic carrier analysis (ST3GG). Read-only statistical inspection of a PNG carrier -- LSB ratios, chi-square anomalies, channel stats. `analyze --json` emits a receipt keyed on sha256 of the carrier bytes (the seam join key). The decode/payload path is intentionally NOT exposed.

capabilities.label: Analyze a carrier image for steganographic anomalies (read-only, no payload decode)
capabilities.evidence: C:/Users/bruke/tools/ST3GG/cli.py:419 analyze command (--json branch); steg_core.py:815-913 analyze_image reads no payload
capabilities.notes: Read-only: runs analyze_image (carrier statistics) only and prints {tool,op,file,sha256,analysis}. sha256 is of the carrier file bytes -- ST3GG's own CRC32 lives on the decode path, which is a code-injection channel and is deliberately UNexposed here. Not on PATH (console script 'stegg'), so argv[0] is the absolute venv python + absolute cli.py.
